entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 22 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-22 11:28AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • AFL 2022 round 19 LIVE updates: Tigers, Dockers battle in fierce battle; Martin aggravates hamstring injury
  • Daughter jailed for five years after father begged her to end his life
  • Double murderer makes bid for parole, despite missing evidence
  • Queensland housing shortage prompts review of Airbnb-style rentals
  • ‘The response has been unreal’: Cafe resorts to desperate measure to find staff
  • Rethink on Roma Street Games venue amid congestion concerns
  • The seven management secrets of Anna Wintour
  • Gabba Olympic funding up in air, despite purported pact with former PM
  • Hunt’s heroics keep Dragons in fight for finals
  • Bunny bargain: Souths’ bill for Latrell’s US trip revealed
  • Community in shock after shooting leaves three men dead near Warrnambool
  • The two Isaacs, and the Swans’ Big Buddy
  • Strict new airport biosecurity screening for foot and mouth
  • Morrison government ‘placed pressure’ on Home Affairs over election-day boat intercept
  • Major banks expect official interest rates around 3 per cent by year-end
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • Best of cartoons, July 22, 2022
  • Look who’s back in town: MPs have plenty on their plate as parliament returns
  • Unions want the right to work from home included in enterprise agreements
  • Infrastructure Australia board members step down ahead of review
  • Poorer young Australians suffer world-leading hit to housing: OECD
  • Foreign investors slugged in first step towards Labor budget repair
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • Cartoons of the week
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • Coroner calls for Medicare for prisoners after Indigenous man dies of ear infection
  • Scout leader set to ‘die in jail’ after abusing boys for decades
  • Waste company fined more than $500,000 after worker killed by reversing skip loader
  • Prize-winning high-rise faces wrecking ball after ‘administrative error’
  • ‘We’re not garbage’: A Sydney team’s ambitious plan to use a submarine to salvage a refugee wreck
  • Public schools face funding shortfall as parent contributions drop
  • Recipient of COVID fine tells of confusion as he challenges ticket in court
  • Pothole plague: rain-damaged roads trigger thousands of calls for help
  • Mask mandates are divisive but necessary to protect for all of us
  • In the Herald: July 22, 1987
  • Sri Lankan army raids protest camp, demonstrators beaten
  • China campaigns against AUKUS as Joko Widodo prepares to visit Beijing
  • ‘He refused to defend our nation’: Committee told Trump watched violent mob attack the Capitol
  • Ukraine war crimes investigation epitomises quality journalism
  • January 6 Committee hearings as it happened: Former White House aides to give evidence about Capitol riots
  • Beloved monarch butterflies now two flutters from extinction
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • ‘Russia can be defeated’: Ukraine tries to make the case that it can win the war
  • BBC apologises to royal nanny over false Prince Charles affair allegations
  • Joe Biden tests positive for COVID, experiencing mild symptoms
  • Shock twist as Perth Judge delays sentencing of teacher murder plotter over Banksia Hill concerns
  • Greek archbishop sues pensioners, critics for defamation
  • $3m a month: Court documents reveal cost of locking up sick refugees in city motel
  • As it happened: Morrison government ‘sabotaged protocols’ with election day leak; Splendour in the Grass cancels first day of festival amid heavy rainfall; US Ambassador Caroline Kennedy in Sydney
  • Man to remain in custody after facing court charged with raping girl, 11
  • The end of negative interest rates will not save Europe
  • IAG says disasters, inflation will push up insurance premiums
  • Goodman taps into building supply boom with Brickworks deal
  • ‘A paradigm shift’: Australian miners prosper as Ford inks agreements to power EV transition
  • The Wrap: ASX notches up best week in four months
  • Record number of appeals lodged against Woodside’s 50-year gas project extension
  • Wealth boom masks big gaps between haves and have-nots
  • Elon Musk adds $25b to his fortune in one day as Tesla surges
  • Land scramble leads to surging prices for industrial sites
  • Xi has made China a pariah for global investors
  • Ex-Coinbase manager charged in crypto insider trading case
  • European Central Bank raises interest rates for first time in 11 years
  • ‘Cognitive dissonance’: Lean times loom for start-ups after bull market mania
  • ‘I felt ready to tell the story’: The trauma behind Del Kathryn Barton’s new movie
  • ‘I’m looking forward to being cancelled so I don’t have to make another movie’
  • Splendour in the Grass organisers vow to push on after first day cancelled
  • Contradictions in the life and work of an Australian original
  • How learning Italian and moving to Rome changed Jhumpa Lahiri’s life
  • Going round in crop circles with a zany pair of tricksters
  • ‘The grimmest night of my life’: Rain causes chaos at Splendour in the Grass
  • My dad sparked my love of space. I wish he saw the James Webb photos
  • ‘I’m bloody homesick’: The movie that made Christos Tsiolkas cry for Melbourne
  • Paul Capsis dares reveal his naked voice
  • So long, Paulie: Real wiseguy brought swagger and menace to The Sopranos
  • What to read: The Winston Churchill cult and mouth-watering food discoveries
  • What Couples Therapy Australia can teach us about our relationships
  • The great COVID-19 gap: Why is pop culture ignoring the pandemic?
  • Google’s budget phone delivers the same smarts with lower-priced parts
  • To save Australia’s precious environment we must do these five things
  • Improving the environment and delivering economic and social wins
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 22 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-22 11:26AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong protests: teens set to face jail time for rioting
  • Loss-making deals in Hong Kong’s secondary home market jump as sellers take a hit from rising rates, emigration wave
  • Hong Kong child abuse scandal: ex-carer jailed for about 7 months, becoming second person sentenced over case linked to charity group
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong ‘could experience 5-digit daily caseloads’ if more transmissive Omicron subvariants become dominant; 4,270 cases logged
  • Hong Kong customs confiscates cannabis worth HK$30 million, arrests suspected triad member
  • Hong Kong government reveals latest line-up of undersecretaries and political assistants, with only 2 deputies to top officials picked from outside administration
  • Stay away from ‘destructive forces’ in industry, John Lee warns Hong Kong journalists at media awards ceremony
  • Two thirds of mainland customers in Greater Bay Area want to buy insurance products in Hong Kong, survey shows
  • As Hong Kong swelters, offices set thermostats higher to fight climate change, but allow staff to dress down (a little)
  • Hong Kong teenager who kept up studies despite cancer diagnosis among those honoured in Student of the Year award
  • How will Hong Kong respond to crises? Revamped home and youth affairs bureau promises volunteer network, emergency stockpiles for quick community action
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong hospital’s data dashboard cuts patient discharge time by a third
  • China’s Pinduoduo quietly expands e-commerce operations in Hong Kong, wooing consumers with bargains and direct shipping
  • Covid-19 has only reinforced the need for better healthcare in Hong Kong. John Lee must ride the reform wave
  • Hong Kong protests: prosecutors press more serious charges against suspects for carrying zip ties to 2019 clashes
  • Fewer ferries lead to more Chinese white dolphin activity, Hong Kong group finds
  • Secondary schools in Hong Kong close ranks to appeal for class size reduction amid fall in pupil numbers
  • Hong Kong government ‘vehemently condemns’ US lawmakers’ call for sanctions on city’s prosecutors, says it shows disrespect for rule of law
  • Preventing heatstroke: Hong Kong government to include heat index in guidelines for employers to shield outdoor workers from high temperatures
  • Nanjing punishes officials for temple honouring Japanese war criminals
  • The last giants are gone: Chinese paddlefish and Yangtze sturgeon declared extinct despite conservation efforts
  • Timeline: how Didi went from poster child for China tech to cautionary tale of the risks incurred by defying Beijing
  • China considers further easing quarantine rules as it seeks to reduce economic damage from Covid curbs
  • Chinese scientists have found a rice gene that can boost yield and shorten the growth cycle
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping wishes Joe Biden a speedy recovery from Covid-19
  • China’s focus on gang violence after Tangshan attack does little to protect women
  • Joe Biden schedules summit with African leaders as US renews push to invest in the continent
  • China reporter cyberbullied over Abe tears in reported suicide attempt
  • Ukraine war could deepen China-Russia military ties, Japan defence white paper says
  • Chinese companies to kick off global depositary receipt sales on SIX Swiss Exchange as regulator grants conditional approval for Keda Industrial
  • Australia is risking its own neck for the US in the South China Sea – has it stopped to ask why?
  • Indonesian leader Joko Widodo to meet Xi Jinping in China next week
  • To avoid China-Taiwan military conflict, send Xi clear message on consequences: British spy chief
  • Will South Korea’s Indo-Pacific strategy ‘imitate’ US to counter China’s regional influence?
  • Why China’s Gen Z see climate change as less important than their Western peers
  • Beijing warns US of ‘forceful’ reply to Taiwan visit by Pelosi
  • China smartphone brands such as Xiaomi continue to lead India market despite increased scrutiny, Canalys data shows
  • US scientists keep ties with Chinese peers despite crackdown: researcher
  • EU wants good ties with Beijing, but will still stand with Taiwan, top lawmaker says
  • Tencent to shut down Chinese NFT platform Huanhe a year after launch, report says, amid declining market
  • Chinese scientists unveil high-powered laser that can ‘write’ in the air
  • Two finalists in race for British PM unlikely to lead to big changes in Sino-UK relations
  • China’s banking crisis draws fresh scrutiny as economic slowdown highlights emerging risks
  • South Korea restores military drills once reduced to help Trump – gets tough with North Korea, bolsters ties with US
  • Japan outlines concerns over increased China-Russia cooperation amid Ukraine war
  • Japan regulator says release of treated Fukushima water can go ahead, locals and China worried
  • Taxpayer-funded state funeral for Japan’s Shinzo Abe triggers protests
  • East Timor’s President Jose Ramos-Horta hopes Indonesia will aid ‘symbolic’ Asean entry in 2023
  • Sri Lanka economic crisis: Dinesh Gunawardena becomes new PM as security forces dismantle protest camp
  • India elects Droupadi Murmu as president, its first from a tribal community
  • Thailand confirms first monkeypox infection on day WHO meets to decide global health emergency
  • Biden and Trump: a tale of 2 presidents with Covid-19
  • ‘It’s a massacre’: police raid on Rio favela kills at least 18, sparking anger
  • January 6 panel lays out Donald Trump’s ‘187 minutes of inaction’ when he watched TV in dining room
  • Ukraine war must end to prevent nuclear ‘abyss’, Belarus strongman Lukashenko says
  • Ukraine’s Zelensky hopes to turn tide of war with Russia as US considers sending fighter jets
  • US records first polio case in almost a decade, and patient has developed paralysis
  • Vladimir Putin and Saudi crown prince talk oil, just days after Joe Biden’s Middle East trip
  • Beloved monarch butterflies now listed as endangered, one step closer to extinction
  • Italy to hold snap election after PM Mario Draghi quits
  • Joe Biden tests positive for Covid-19, ‘very mild symptoms’, White House says
  • BBC pays damages to former royal nanny over ‘false and malicious’ claims when she worked for Princess Diana
  • Vatican’s magnificent art belongs to us say Canada’s indigenous groups
  • Russian bourse records US$11 million worth of trades in Alibaba, other Hong Kong-listed stocks in first trial month
  • Video: Heatwaves and wildfires engulf parts of US and western Europe
  • Video: Going underground to beat the summer heat in parts of China
  • How emerging market currencies are weathering US dollar surge
  • Hong Kong stocks cap best week in July as China pledges to stem housing rot, Alibaba and Meituan lead tech winners
  • ECB surprises with aggressive hike, first since 2011, to end the era of negative interest rates in eurozone
  • A fifth of MNCs in China rethink office space leasing plans amid tough Covid-19 curbs, while 4 in 100 cancel expansion
  • What does Hong Kong’s move to cut out lawyers in mortgage transactions mean for property buyers
  • Chinese Communist Party branch formed in HSBC onshore venture
  • Central banks forced to continue raising interest rates despite slowdown after months of dithering
  • Britain blocks Chinese company’s deal to acquire camera technology as it could pose risk to national security
  • Chinese e-cigarette giant gets tobacco production licence, gaining foothold in country’s vaping market
  • As China’s mortgage boycott revolt spreads, senior banking regulator assures steps to solve crisis
  • How sunburn harms our skin, often years later, and how to avoid it: apply sunscreen – SPF 30 or SPF 50, depending on what you’re doing
  • How Hong Kong restaurant Haku’s interior design was inspired by its eclectic menu and music playlist
  • Attention, K-pop fans: girl group NewJeans announce their arrival with music video that’s an instant YouTube hit
  • International arts collaboration via Zoom a new model forged in the coronavirus pandemic; Hong Kong dance troupe is a beneficiary
  • Just 1 Day movie review: Charlene Choi, Wong Cho-lam play would-be lovers in a romantic trifle – Erica Li’s directing debut
  • An NFT collection of food or monsters? Artist says it’s both – he finds the ‘beasts’ in mirrored images of dishes, from dumplings to French toast
  • The best dumplings in Hong Kong, a Times Square go-to and a filmmaker’s pick for fried chicken
  • In Disney+ series Pistol, the Sex Pistols, UK punk rock pioneers, are back on TV 45 years on from God Save The Queen
  • His story is also the story of Hong Kong: Alan Chan, designer, on an East-meets-West career
  • 10 years after Psy’s Gangnam Style, has a K-pop song ever hit in the same way?
  • Why are residential resort properties booming? After Covid-19, homebuyers are looking to invest in real estate with five-star amenities in Hawaii, the Bahamas and Vietnam
  • On Her Watch: How new materials are revolutionising watchmaking, as Chanel, Rolex, Van Cleef & Arpels, Omega and more brands create exciting women’s timepieces with modern elements and techniques
  • Who is Lalit Modi, the businessman dating Sushmita Sen? The founder of the US$1 billion Indian Premier League just announced on Instagram that he’s in love … but had to flee India in 2010
  • 20 celebrity kids who are BFFs: from Kim Kardashian and Jessica Simpson’s daughters, to J. Lo and Ben Affleck’s teens and Beyoncé’s Blue Ivy and Apple Martin, these stars’ mini-mes are bonding
  • What did Elvis Presley and Caitlyn Jenner have in common? Meet Linda Thompson, the King of Rock ‘n’ Roll’s girlfriend after Priscilla, who married the Kardashians star – but was left out of the biopic
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Washington Post: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russian and Israeli planes hit Syria in separate strikes, kill at least 10
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Grain deal set to be signed in Turkey; U.K. says Russia could ‘run out of steam’
  • Russia about to ‘run out of steam’ in Ukraine, U.K. intelligence chief says
  • A drought in Italy’s risotto heartland is killing the rice
  • Sri Lanka raids protest camp, as allies of ousted leader reconsolidate power
  • Police raid kills 18 in Rio, sparking outcry and accusations of abuse
  • Thousands of frogs are dying in Australia. Scientists aren’t sure why.
  • War narrows the many divides between east and west in Ukraine
  • The apocalyptic vision behind Putin’s ‘golden billion’ argument
  • Ukraine live briefing: Moscow, Kyiv reach deal on blocked grain, Turkey says; E.U. raises interest rates to tackle inflation
  • Russia’s moves in Africa problematic for U.S. interests, general agrees
  • Italy’s Mario Draghi resigns; new elections are set for September
  • The world’s oldest known male panda, beloved in Hong Kong, dies at 35
  • European Central Bank takes big step to address rising inflation fears
  • In a first, India elects tribal woman to be president
  • Biden: U.S. military does not support Nancy Pelosi visiting Taiwan
  • How Russia is laying the groundwork for its annexation of Ukraine
  • Russia resumes gas flows to Germany after Nord Stream maintenance
  • Ukraine’s first lady says her son, once into arts, now wants to be a soldier
  • Russia and Syria conducted dozens of illegal ‘double tap’ strikes, report says
  • Deal for Ukraine grain exports due to be sealed in Istanbul
  • Jihadi rebels attack key military base near Mali's capital
  • Judge says Prince Harry can sue UK govt over security plan
  • Women climbers from Pakistan and Iran reach K2 summit
  • UK blames France as travelers face hours-long port delays
  • ‘Run Hawley Run’: Video of Josh Hawley fleeing Jan. 6 rioters sparks memes
  • Pat Benatar won’t play ‘Hit Me With Your Best Shot’ after mass shootings
  • She won the lottery. Then she shared her windfall with strangers.
  • Shonka Dukureh, blues singer and ‘Elvis’ actress, found dead at 44
  • N.Y. GOP governor nominee Zeldin attacked at campaign event, says he’s fine
  • Lightning strike kills one soldier, injures nine at Georgia base
  • A U.K. meteorologist warned of deadly heat. He was told to cheer up.
  • Trump White House aide goes on sexist tirade, calls Jan. 6 panel ‘anti-White’
  • Bannon closings arguments Friday, jury deliberations likely to follow
  • Agents protecting Pence feared ‘for their own lives,’ official says
  • ‘I don’t want to say the election’s over.’ Trump dug in after Jan. 6 attack.
  • Trump ‘chose not to act’ as mob terrorized the Capitol, panel shows
  • What we know — and don’t know — about what Trump did on Jan. 6
  • Biden’s bout with covid tests his return-to-normal strategy
  • Officers describe assault on Jan. 6 in front of accused attacker
  • Steve Bannon trial closing arguments set for Friday
  • Post Politics Now: ‘Folks, I’m doing great,’ Biden says after positive coronavirus test
  • Closing arguments in Steve Bannon’s trial set for Friday
  • Why Amazon is buying a little-known medical provider for $3.9 billion
  • The FBI says it busted a big crypto Ponzi scam. It just got bigger.
  • The job market is beginning to show cracks
  • How Gen Z teens accidentally blew up the myth of the lazy millennial
  • Mortgage rates pause ahead of next week’s Federal Reserve meeting
  • China fines Didi $1.2 billion for breaking data-security laws
  • South Carolina bill outlaws websites that tell how to get an abortion
  • Amazon just bought my doctor’s office. That makes me very nervous.
  • Historical Howard County, Md., farm lists for $4 million
  • D.C. family peels away older renovations and unveils dream home
  • Prosecuting Trump Has Only Become More Urgent
  • Assuming You’ll Retire Healthy Is an Expensive Mistake
  • The Fed Is Failing in Four Ways
  • The Populist Experiment in Italy Has Failed
  • Why China’s Developers Are Facing Mortgage Boycotts: QuickTake
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How Sri Lanka Landed in a Crisis and What It Means: QuickTake
  • Trump’s behavior on Jan. 6 was worse than you thought. Much worse.
  • The pro-life movement needs to move cautiously in purple states
  • China wants to ‘reduce misunderstanding’ with the U.S. It could start by talking.
  • This Jan. 6 hearing was the best showbiz yet. Will it matter? Hmm.
  • Your questions about covid-19, answered by Dr. Leana Wen
  • How Trump violated his oath on Jan. 6 by doing nothing
  • 5 things we learned in the Jan. 6 committee’s stunning hearing
  • Why Europe is tougher than you thought
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Jan. 6 Panel Presents Evidence of Trump’s Refusal to Stop the Riot
  • 5 Takeaways From the Eighth Hearing of the Jan. 6 Committee
  • Video: Josh Hawley Cheered on Jan. 6. Then He Fled.
  • Service Service Asked to Stop Investigating Jan. 6 Texts Amid Criminal Inquiry
  • The Economy Putin Didn’t Actually Ruin
  • Republicans Sharpen Post-Roe Attacks on L.G.B.T.Q. Rights
  • House Passes Bill to Ensure Contraception Rights After Dobbs
  • Lee Zeldin, Republican Candidate for N.Y. Governor, Is Attacked
  • Newsom Wants Democrats to Fight Fire With Fire, Starting With a Gun Bill
  • In ‘Uncoupled,’ Neil Patrick Harris Plays the Game
  • What Can You Travel With in Your Carry-On Luggage?
  • The Future Is Italy, and It’s Bleak
  • Is Life a Story or a Game?
  • The Electoral College Makes a Coup Very Tempting
  • A Top Mental Health Expert on Where America Went Wrong
  • Religious Doctrine, Not the Constitution, Drove the Dobbs Decision
  • President Biden Has Covid. Now What?
  • Setting the Record Straight on Covid Variants and Reinfections
  • Cheaper Gas
  • Utah’s ‘Environmental Nuclear Bomb’
  • Eggplant Five Ways
  • How to Ease Neck and Back Pain From Travel
  • Court Win Bolsters Push to Highlight Prosecutors’ Misconduct
  • ​South Korea’s New President Calls for Criminal Investigation of Past Government
  • Supreme Court Refuses for Now to Restore Biden Plan on Immigration Enforcement
  • Ukraine Live Updates: Turkey Promises a Deal to Get Grain Out of Blocked Ports
  • Sri Lankan Forces Raid Protest Camp
  • The Race to Succeed Boris Johnson, Explained
  • After Mocking France’s Literary Elite, a Fraught Invite Into the Club
  • Ukraine Tries to Make the Case That It Can Win the War
  • Deep in a Covid Wave, Europe Counts Cases and Carries On
  • Italy’s President Accepts Draghi Resignation, Calling for New Elections
  • India’s Next President Will Make History When She’s Sworn In
  • Russia Restarts Gas Shipments Through Pipeline, but Keeps Germany Guessing
  • BBC Apologizes to Royal Nanny Swept Up in Diana Reporting Furor
  • Belgium Ratifies Prisoner-Exchange Treaty With Iran
  • 1947: Deadly Volcanic Eruption in Nicaragua Menaces Nearby City
  • As Covid Cases Rise Again, No Clear Guidance From the Top
  • Your Friday Briefing
  • Russia Moves to Close Agency Handling Emigration to Israel
  • Putin Is ‘Entirely Too Healthy,’ C.I.A. Director Says
  • Trees Dedicated to Holocaust Victims Are Destroyed at Buchenwald
  • Your Friday Briefing: Ukraine Launches a Southern Counteroffensive
  • Biden Has Begun Taking Paxlovid, an Antiviral Medication
  • Vice President Kamala Harris Tests Negative
  • A Painful Deadline Nears as Colorado River Reservoirs Run Critically Low
  • Risk From Covid-19 Is Increasing in Much of the U.S.
  • In Washington, Olena Zelenska Dressed for Ukraine
  • Judge Criticizes Georgia Prosecutor for Aiding the Political Rival of a Trump Ally
  • He Built a Home to Survive a Civil War. Tragedy Found Him Anyway.
  • Falling Tree Kills 2 and Injures 3 During Storm in Alabama
  • Outtakes From a Video Show Trump Refusing to Abandon a Lie
  • Melania Trump: ‘I Was Unaware’ of Jan. 6 Violence
  • Who Is Sarah Matthews?
  • Who Is Matthew Pottinger?
  • The Hole in the Center of American Politics
  • Is Trump Watching the Jan. 6 Hearings?
  • Train Fire Outside Boston Prompts Evacuation, and a Leap Into the Mystic River
  • After Tonight’s Hearing, What’s Next for the Jan 6. Committee?
  • Police Officer Dies After Medical Emergency in Louisiana Heat
  • Bennie Thompson to Preside Over Jan. 6 Hearing Remotely After Positive Covid Test
  • Jill Biden Keeps Schedule Despite Close Contact With President
  • Lawmakers Who Traveled With Biden Are at Capitol But Testing and Masking
  • China Fines Didi $1.2 Billion as Tech Sector Pressures Persist
  • What Is the Yield Curve? Wall Street’s Recession Alarm Is Ringing.
  • ECB Rates: Europe Joins Inflation Fight With First Increase in 11 Years
  • Amazon Wants 100,000 Electric Vans. Can Rivian Deliver?
  • Ex-Coinbase Employee and 2 Others Charged With Insider Trading of Crypto Assets
  • Amazon to Acquire One Medical Clinics in $39 Billion Deal
  • Loans Could Burn Start-Up Workers in Downturn
  • How Software Is Stifling Competition and Slowing Innovation
  • Germany Takes a Stake in Struggling Gas Provider Uniper
  • When a Woman’s Retirement Account Becomes the Family Emergency Fund
  • Trial of Ex-Twitter Employee Accused of Spying for Saudis Begins
  • Snap Reports User Growth But a Wider Loss in the Second Quarter
  • Mattel Earnings: Toymaker Reports a 20 Percent Jump in Sales
  • Truckers’ Protests Over Labor Law Block Access to Oakland’s Port
  • YouTube will take down ‘unsafe’ abortion how-to videos.
  • The ECB Has a New Tool to Keep Bond Markets in Check: the TPI
  • Meta tweaks Facebook app to act more like TikTok.
  • Ford Increases Battery Supply to Meet Electric Vehicle Goals
  • Decision Day for the E.C.B.
  • American Airlines has its best quarter of revenue ever, but profit is down from 2019.
  • What to Know About Penn Station’s $7 Billion Redevelopment Plan
  • 8 Ultraviolet Wands Could Pose Danger of Radiation Injury, F.D.A. Warns
  • Reporter Says Taliban Forced Her to Publicly Retract Accurate Articles
  • You Can Be Both Dull and Innovative
  • Fox News Will Not Carry Thursday’s Jan. 6 Hearing Live
News from the New York Times

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Global equity funds see biggest weekly outflow in five weeks
  • China's Xi Jinping offers Sri Lanka's new president support amid crisis
  • China says will make great efforts to consolidate economic recovery
  • Global steel output falls 5.9% yr/yr in June -WorldSteel
  • Taiwanese singer Jimmy Lin injured after his Tesla crashes; car burst into flames
  • Brentford bolster defence by signing free agent Mee
  • China urges to promote bond market to support private enterprises
  • Astana suspend Lopez over alleged link to drug trafficking investigation
  • 'No intention of moving': Jetstar says it is 'extremely disappointed' with Changi Airport's decision to relocate airline to T4
  • Changes will be made to COE quota system from August to reduce supply volatility: LTA
  • China will require online ride hailing firms to submit real-time data
  • Changi Airport's Terminal 4 to reopen on Sep 13
  • Despite Didi's US$1.2 billion fine, China tech's regulatory woes may not be over
  • Kenya's first female professional gamer fights to define herself on her terms
  • What should you do if a cockroach crawls inside your ear? Don’t try to get it out yourself, says a doctor
  • Global brands are taking note of Africa's music and talent
  • Ten Hag keen to bolster attacking options before transfer window shuts
  • Xi Jinping sends sympathy message to Joe Biden over COVID-19 infection
  • China criticises Japan's defence white paper for making 'accusations'
  • Indonesia seeks to crack down on "unhealthy" peer-to-peer lenders
  • Chinese Paddlefish and wild Yangtze Sturgeon extinct: IUCN
  • Thailand confirms first monkeypox case in Phuket but patient is missing
  • Japan regulator approves release of treated Fukushima water
  • Japan defence report sounds alarm on Russia, threats to Taiwan
  • Crisis-hit Sri Lanka swears in new prime minister
  • Proud Sri Lanka athletes defy crisis to fulfil Games dreams
  • Indonesia's Jokowi to meet Xi Jinping during upcoming visit to Beijing
  • South Korea, US to resume major field training during combined military drills
  • China to extend anti-dumping duties on steel product from Japan, South Korea, and EU
  • Japan plans Sep 27 state funeral for Shinzo Abe
  • Heatwaves to hit China from east to west as almanac's 'big heat' day looms
  • Security forces smash Sri Lanka's main protest camp
  • Commentary: Property crisis presents China with a dilemma
  • Commentary: China’s new aircraft carrier will alter the balance of power in regional waters
  • Tribal candidate Droupadi Murmu wins Indian presidential election: Partial results
  • Asia's Q2 cocoa grind up 3.6% yr/yr at 228,895 tonnes -CAA
  • Honda to cut output by up to 30% at Japan plants on supply snag
  • Contract workers striking at South Korea's Daewoo shipyard reach agreement - Yonhap
  • Petronas payout to Malaysia govt seen higher at 55-59 billion rgt this year
  • Japan to cut this fiscal year's economic growth forecast - Nikkei
  • Northvolt and Stora Enso to develop batteries from forestry byproduct
  • Tietoevry mulls spin-off of banking unit
  • Hong Kong interbank rate touches 2-year high as HKMA withdraws cash
  • Indonesia considering scrapping domestic sales rule for palm oil exports
  • A year on, China's CO2 market yet to drive big emission cuts
  • Schindler cuts 2022 revenue guidance on China slowdown
  • Malaysia's June CPI up 3.4% y/y, higher than forecast
  • Thai central bank pledges gradual monetary policy adjustments
  • Singtel says it has reached more than 95% 5G coverage nationwide
  • Five on Friday: 5 weird things people have tried to slip past airport security
  • Driver gets jail for causing motorcyclist's death while turning into Yishun car park
  • Appointments needed for COVID-19 vaccination at polyclinics as acute respiratory infection cases rise
  • 343 people under investigation over scams involving more than S$6 million
  • Black Eyed Peas, TLC, The Kid Laroi and Suede join Singapore F1 Grand Prix main stage acts
  • Singapore's private home prices rise by 3.5% in Q2, five times the increase in previous quarter
  • Lorry driver charged with helping fugitive flee Singapore after he allegedly failed to deliver luxury goods

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
